pi_items.source: Verified in: (1) task.json description field states 'write it to a file path `/var/jenkins_home/audit_report.txt`', (2) export_result.sh checks for file at this exact path inside the container, (3) README.md section 4 specifies '/var/jenkins_home/audit_report.txt' as the required file location, (4) setup_task.sh attempts cleanup at this path

Verified in: (1) setup_jenkins.sh line 9 sets ADMIN_USER='admin', (2) task_utils.sh line 6 sets JENKINS_USER='admin', (3) README.md section 'Login Credentials' lists 'Username: admin', (4) task.json description confirms 'Username: admin'

Verified in: (1) setup_jenkins.sh line 10 sets ADMIN_PASS='Admin123!', (2) task_utils.sh line 7 sets JENKINS_PASS='Admin123!', (3) README.md section 'Login Credentials' lists 'Password: Admin123!', (4) task.json description confirms 'Password: Admin123!', (5) export_result.sh line 60 uses this password for API calls

privileged_info_summary: All three metadata claims for this task have been independently verified against multiple authoritative sources. The login credentials are username 'admin' with password 'Admin123!' (confirmed in setup_jenkins.sh, task_utils.sh, README.md, and task.json). The audit report file is expected to be written to /var/jenkins_home/audit_report.txt inside the Jenkins container (confirmed in task description, export_result.sh verification script, README.md, and setup_task.sh). The task uses synthetic test data: setup_task.sh creates three fictional Jenkins jobs (webapp-frontend-build, api-integration-tests, release-deploy-pipeline) as test fixtures for the audit task. The Jenkins environment runs via Docker using the jenkins/jenkins:lts-jdk21 image as specified in docker-compose.yml.
